bead.title: Finish wiring the Vidar DS4 MTP benchmark lane
bead.description:
Problem
fiz-vidar-ds4-mtp is a concrete example of a half-wired lane. The sweep plan has a lane entry, but the matching profile file is absent, recipes do not include the lane, wrapper aliases do not map ds4-mtp to the lane id, and FIZEAU_DS4_MTP is only a placeholder unless the ds4 provider/server actually consumes it.

In scope
- Add scripts/benchmark/profiles/vidar-ds4-mtp.yaml or generate it through the new lane authoring workflow.
- Enroll fiz-vidar-ds4-mtp in the intended recipes after confirming which recipes should compare MTP-on versus MTP-off; at minimum include the timing/comparison recipe used for the MTP experiment.
- Add a short alias such as ds4-mtp/vidar-ds4-mtp if alias handling remains user-facing.
- Confirm the real DS4 MTP enablement mechanism and wire it through the Fizeau DS4 provider, benchmark profile metadata, or server startup notes as appropriate. Remove or rename the placeholder FIZEAU_DS4_MTP if it is not the actual switch.

Out of scope
- Full TerminalBench run.
- Claiming MTP performance deltas before runtime_props or server evidence confirms MTP was active.
bead.acceptance:
1. `go test ./cmd/bench -run TestLoadSweepPlan -count=1` passes and the plan/profile validator accepts fiz-vidar-ds4-mtp.
2. `scripts/benchmark/run_terminalbench_2_1_sweep.sh --phase timing-baseline --lanes ds4-mtp --dry-run` reaches the dry-run plan for fiz-vidar-ds4-mtp instead of failing alias, recipe, or profile validation.
3. `rg -n "FIZEAU_DS4_MTP|mtp" internal/provider scripts/benchmark/profiles scripts/benchmark/terminalbench-2-1-sweep.yaml` shows either the real implemented switch or comments explaining server-side enablement, with no unverified placeholder presented as operational.
4. One can run an ad-hoc subset dry run with `--subset terminalbench-2-1-canary --lanes fiz-vidar-ds4-mtp` without profile-not-found errors.
